channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Dumped fridge freezer been there months, 13th March
item.description:
Dumped fridge freezer

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ravenscroft Crescent, Mottingham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Deep pothole, hidden by non-draining gutter, 13th March
item.description:
This pothole has been previously reported and marked as "fixed". However the drain is still not draining, and the pothole is deep enough to cause damage to vehicles or unseat a bicycle rider

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 161, Station Road, Longlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 1 metre long by 20cm wide hole in road/pavement, 13th March
item.description:
The hole was first reported a couple of years age and was eventually repaired badly. The hole has got much bigger and is starting to sink. It is in front of my drive and I have to drive over/through the hole whenever I use my car.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Widmore Road, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Yellow line missing, 13th March
item.description:
The road in Danson Crescent has been resurfaced and is looking very good. Prior to these works, there was a yellow line between house numbers 12 - 14 (opposite 43). This was so useful as there are so many parked cars in the road, it is often difficult for me to get off my drive. The yellow line has not been replaced. Is it planned to happen?
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Danson Crescent, Welling, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

.

Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead Fox, 13th March
item.description:
As you do not provide a service to remove dead pets from your home or property or garden. We have followed your guidance and placed the wild dead animal (fox) in two sealed bags at the edge of your property and are now reporting it to you. The dead fox is on the corner of the driveway of 150 Mead Way, in two sealed black sacks.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Mead Way, Hayes, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dog poo, 13th March
item.description:
Again dog garden with dog mess

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 6-8, Toussaint Walk, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Vehicle crashed on road, abandoned and dangerous, 13th March
item.description:
Vehicle crashed into post a few weeks ago, there was a domestic incident between passenger and driver. The rear window was smashed. Vehicle was turned around to face the other way and slowly over the weeks bits of the car have been taken off it, this week the car has been put on a jack and a wheel and suspension removed. It’s very dangerous to children and animals as it could claspe from the jack. Believe it belongs to 1 smith court but they show no attempt to remove the vehicle from the road

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Fordcroft Road, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside No 21/23, 13th March
item.description:
Road signs warning vehicles that there is a penalty for passing through to the rest of Harmood Street and that there is a camera have been defaced and vehicles are driving through as they can’t see what is on the sign.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 39, Harmood Street, Chalk Farm, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
